mechanism_narrative: Stronger trailing 3-month PAYEMS growth raises expected household income and aggregate demand, so higher payroll momentum predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the 3-month PAYEMS growth signal has a walk-forward Rank IC below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window or if top-quintile PAYEMS momentum does not outperform bottom-quintile momentum over 20 trading days.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in UNRATE marks deterioration in labor income and recession risk, causing equity investors to reduce expected earnings; when activated, it predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE 3-month change crosses above +0.3 percentage points
failure_condition: Reject if triggered instances produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of walk-forward observations or if mean triggered returns are not below unconditional mean returns at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A downward shift in FEDFUNDS reduces discount rates and financing costs, increasing the present value of equity cash flows; a material easing trigger predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS 3-month change crosses below -0.25 percentage points
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle outside severe credit stress
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward returns after FEDFUNDS easing triggers are not higher than unconditional 60-day returns in walk-forward tests or if the hit rate is below 50% for two consecutive 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent strength in RSAFS and HOUST indicates broad household demand and residential investment expansion, improving earnings breadth; the joint signal predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint positive RSAFS-HOUST trigger fails to outperform cases where only one proxy is positive, or if triggered 60-day mean returns are not above unconditional returns at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A downside break in INDPRO indicates shrinking industrial output and margin pressure for cyclical firms; when production momentum turns negative, it predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO level crosses below its trailing 6-month moving average and 3-month change is negative
failure_condition: Reject if triggered INDPRO breakdowns have a nonnegative average 20-day excess return versus unconditional returns or if the negative signal hit rate falls below 45% over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation acceleration paired with a rising policy rate tightens real financial conditions and compresses equity valuation multiples; the joint CPIAUCSL-FEDFUNDS signal predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation rises by more than 1 percentage point and FEDFUNDS 3-month change > 0
failure_condition: Reject if the interaction term adds no explanatory power beyond separate CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS effects in walk-forward tests or if triggered 5-day returns are positive in more than 60% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Higher trailing GDPC1 growth reflects stronger real aggregate demand and corporate revenue capacity, so stronger real GDP momentum predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if real-time GDPC1 momentum has a walk-forward Rank IC below 0.03 for 60-day returns over any 4-year window or if high-growth GDP quarters do not outperform low-growth quarters.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly decline in RSAFS indicates an immediate consumption shock that lowers near-term earnings expectations for broad equities; when the drop threshold is crossed, it predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if RSAFS drop triggers do not produce below-median 5-day S&P 500 returns in walk-forward tests or if the conditional mean return is not below the unconditional mean at p < 0.10.
